activeGoal: Fast-track implementation of the human-architect-approved Neo4j chain: add Execute Neo4j Cypher and Neo4j Query Native T
designSummary: Issue-driven full-model refinement for ThreatIntelAnalyst. Canonical analyst flow is updated from `stix_query` to `neo4j_query`; `db_schema_explorer` is refined so its schema authority comes from `agent_app/opencode_app/.opencode/schema/**` as a semantic menu for agent-guided query construction; and the analyst boundary is expanded from query-only evidence lookup to incident-driven threat-intelligence extraction plus idempotent, traceable database writeback. Existing software units remain the correct ownership seams: OpenCode Threat Intelligence Workspace for agent/skill contracts, STIX Schema Explorer Native Tool for schema-menu exposure, and Neo4j Query Native Tool for analyst database interaction. `stix_query` is retained only as transitional compatibility and is no longer the canonical analyst contract.
designDecisions: ADR: Preserve the human-authored Neo4j architecture unchanged for ISSUE-001. The approved ApplicationFunction {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC} and ApplicationComponent {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394} remain authoritative; the issue is traceability/task-contract completeness, not a design defect.

ADR: Accept existing software unit ELM-APP-COMP-STIX-CLI (STIX Semantic Query CLI) as the implementation owner for Execute Neo4j Cypher because the realized behavior already exists in agent_app/opencode_app/tools/stix_cli/semantic_query.py and __main__.py, and the graph already contains the Assignment from ELM-APP-COMP-STIX-CLI to {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC}.

ADR: Reject introducing a new Neo4j-specific software unit or moving ownership into the tool wrapper. The JS tool file agent_app/opencode_app/.opencode/tools/neo4j_query.js correctly remains the agent-facing ApplicationComponent {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394}; it already has tagged tests. The missing QA intent coverage is specifically at ApplicationFunction {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC}, so the minimal repair is tagged tests and task routing anchored to the existing Python CLI unit.

ADR: Do not invent or backfill a Requirement ID for the Neo4j batch because no authoritative requirement element was recoverable from the runtime graph. Route implementation rework using architecture-element traceability and preserve the absence of a requirement link as a known upstream metadata gap rather than fabricating motivation semantics.

ADR: Do not introduce new software units for this mission. Extend existing modules `agent_app/opencode_app/.opencode/agents|skills`, `agent_app/opencode_app/.opencode/tools/db_schema_explorer.js`, and `agent_app/opencode_app/.opencode/tools/neo4j_query.js` because the repository already contains the correct ownership boundaries for analyst contracts, schema exploration, and Neo4j access.

ADR: Promote `neo4j_query` to the canonical ThreatIntelAnalyst database interaction boundary and demote `stix_query` to compatibility-only status. The analyst interface, alias interface, and collaboration skill must no longer require `stix_query` as the primary callable contract.

ADR: Change `db_schema_explorer` schema authority from bundle-derived summary alone to workspace semantic catalog authority sourced from `agent_app/opencode_app/.opencode/schema/**`. Bundle-observed hints may remain supplemental, but the agent-facing menu must be rooted in the repository schema catalog.

ADR: Expand ThreatIntelAnalyst responsibility from read-only evidence lookup to incident-driven extraction plus idempotent, traceable writeback. ThreatIntelSecOps remains downstream assessment-only and must not assume schema lookup or persistence ownership.

ModelUpdated: No architecture-model refinement is required for TASK-003 and TASK-004 after audit triage on commit e7e2d21b46b7ac75ff2269be0ad91fb85b9aafc0. Current repository reality shows intent-tagged verification for ELM-APP-FUNC-PUBLISH-SEMANTIC-SCHEMA-MENU in tests/test_opencode_workspace_config.py line 154 and for ELM-APP-FUNC-CANONICALIZE-THREAT-ANALYST-CONTRACT in lines 376 and 416, while the approved ApplicationFunction contracts and software-unit ownership remain unchanged. Treat the failed audit as a stale validation-timing/traceability snapshot and rerun Audit against current code reality before any further implementation rework.

audit.summary: Architecture compliance audit PASSED on rerun for baseline commit e7e2d21b46b7ac75ff2269be0ad91fb85b9aafc0 against current repository reality in full-model lane. Intent model remains sufficient with no core-layer gap. Current reality confirms architecture alignment for TASK-003/TASK-004/TASK-005: analyst contracts canonicalize to schema-first neo4j_query usage with incident-driven writeback semantics; db_schema_explorer publishes the workspace semantic schema menu from agent_app/opencode_app/.opencode/schema/**; neo4j_query remains the canonical analyst read/write boundary with traceable writeback summaries. Automated tagged verification is now present for intended architecture IDs ELM-APP-FUNC-PUBLISH-SEMANTIC-SCHEMA-MENU (tests/test_opencode_workspace_config.py line 154), ELM-APP-FUNC-CANONICALIZE-THREAT-ANALYST-CONTRACT (lines 376 and 416), and ELM-APP-FUNC-EXECUTE-ANALYST-NEO4J-FLOW. This supersedes the prior failed audit result as a stale validation-timing/traceability snapshot rather than a design gap.
